hrv.sheldonNote: 12ms below 30-day baseline. This is the third consecutive dip. I am monitoring it.

heartRate.sheldonNote: Resting HR elevated 4bpm. Consistent with elevated stress load this week.

recovery.sheldonNote: Below 65 threshold. I have blocked 2 meetings accordingly.

strain.sheldonNote: Strain exceeds recovery capacity. The math does not balance.

sleep.sheldonNote: 6.1 hours is insufficient for your cognitive demands. I have added a sleep protocol to your approvals queue.

activity.sheldonNote: Activity is adequate. However, the intensity-to-recovery ratio is imbalanced.

sheldonInsights: Your HRV has declined 12ms from baseline over 7 days. This is correlated with your decision to schedule back-to-back investor calls on Tuesday.
Sleep staging shows REM compression. The late-screen usage is the most probable cause.
Recovery at 61/100 is below the threshold I use to allow high-cognitive scheduling.
I cross-referenced your glucose data with your decision latency. The pattern is clear: decisions made before 10am on sub-7hr sleep take 2.3x longer and have a higher error rate.
Your strain-to-recovery ratio has been imbalanced for 9 consecutive days. I have a protocol ready.

suggestions.title: Cardiologist appointment — Dr. Sarah Chen
suggestions.description: 4.9★ rating, BlueCross covered, March 3rd 2pm available. Intro email drafted and ready to send. Resting HR trend warrants this check.

suggestions.title: CNS recovery day — cancel Thursday workout
suggestions.description: HRV at 44ms. A training session today increases recovery deficit and impacts Sunday readiness. I recommend replacing with a 20-minute walk.
suggestions.action: Approve to cancel training block + add walk

suggestions.title: Eliminate post-7pm carbohydrates — 14-day trial
suggestions.description: Oura data shows 4.2% deep sleep reduction on nights with late-carb meals. Correlation is statistically significant across 31 tracked nights.

suggestions.title: Weekly decompression block — Fridays 4–5pm
suggestions.description: You've skipped this window 3 consecutive weeks. Correlation with Monday cognitive output is 0.71. The block works. You keep cancelling it.

suggestions.title: Therapy intake — Dr. Marcus Webb
suggestions.description: High-performance anxiety specialist. First session covered by EAP. You mentioned feeling 'scattered' four times last week. I take notes.

timeline.event: Therapy intake approved — Dr. Marcus Webb
Switched to 4-day split + 2 active recovery days

today.label: End of Day
today.description: Wrap-up. Final approvals. No new initiatives.

recipeVault.name: High-Protein Recovery Bowl
recipeVault.smokeNote: Optimal post-training meal. Combines leucine-rich protein with complex carbs for glycogen replenishment without glucose spike.

recipeVault.name: Cognitive Stack Smoothie
recipeVault.smokeNote: Lion's mane + blueberries + MCT oil is not a trend. The research on cognitive performance is compelling. This is your 9am fuel.

recipeVault.name: Sleep-Optimized Dinner
recipeVault.smokeNote: Tryptophan → serotonin → melatonin. The mechanism is established. Eat this before 7pm and your deep sleep percentage will improve within 5 days.

recipeVault.name: Pre-Training Performance Plate
recipeVault.smokeNote: Consumed 90 minutes pre-session. The carb load is intentional — you need glycogen. Do not eat this when your HRV is below 50.

recipeVault.name: Anti-Stress Adaptogen Bowl
recipeVault.smokeNote: Ashwagandha has 14 RCTs supporting cortisol reduction. On elevated-stress days, this is your lunch. Non-negotiable on weeks like this one.
